Our role and fundamental objectives, duty and priorities.

RSH is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government.

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information.

role_appointments.title: Jonathan Walters - Deputy Chief Executive

details.body:
The Chief Executive, Jonathan Walters, is a member of the Board. He leads a team of staff responsible for the regulation of registered social housing providers, in line with the Board’s strategy for regulation.

As accounting officer, the Chief Executive has a direct line of accountability to Parliament.

role_appointments.title: Jonathan Walters - Chief Executive
Kate Dodsworth - Member of Executive team

details.body:
Leading the Regulatory Engagement Directorate, ensuring delivery of effective regulation in order to achieve our statutory objectives, delivering frontline planned and reactive regulation to ensure that registered providers are financially viable, well governed and meet our consumer standards, taking remedial action where necessary.

The CoRE reports to the Chief Executive and plays a key role in leadership and development of the overall strategy at the regulator, driving business improvement and the development of staff and team capacity, and ensuring regulatory approaches are continuously developed.

role_appointments.title: Kate Dodsworth - Chief of Regulatory Engagement

details.body: The Chair of the Board for the Regulator of Social Housing is appointed by and accountable to the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities.

ordered_parent_organisations.title: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

details.body:
The Chief Executive, Jonathan Walters, is a member of the Board. He leads a team of staff responsible for the regulation of registered social housing providers, in line with the Board’s strategy for regulation.

As accounting officer, the Chief Executive has a direct line of accountability to Parliament.
The Chair of the Board for the Regulator of Social Housing is appointed by and accountable to the Secretary of State for Levelling Up, Housing and Communities.

Leading the Regulatory Engagement Directorate, ensuring delivery of effective regulation in order to achieve our statutory objectives, delivering frontline planned and reactive regulation to ensure that registered providers are financially viable, well governed and meet our consumer standards, taking remedial action where necessary.

The CoRE reports to the Chief Executive and plays a key role in leadership and development of the overall strategy at the regulator, driving business improvement and the development of staff and team capacity, and ensuring regulatory approaches are continuously developed.

title: Regulator of Social Housing
